entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content: week 3 completed - ZAC_2229067

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM - API Authentication Methods
entry.content:
Hi Yogananda,

We have an inbound scenario where we have to implement OAuth2 authentication.

We have already set up the Communication System, that resulted in creation of Communication user and a dedicated role.

We are using below URL to fetch token, which is working fine.

GET {{url}}/sap/c4c/api/v1/iam-service/token

 Once we get the token, it is not allowing us to GET Case Details and is giving 403 error.

We understand we need to modify the role generated, we explored communication configuration but it didn't had any scenario for cases.

Alternatively, we have been using Technical user, credentials of which we are using as Client ID and client Secret, although we are able to get case details we are not sure if this is the correct way of using this.

Need your inputs here please.

Thanks and Regards

Sanket Jain

entry.title: Re: Running Hugging Face AI Models with SAPUI5 and CAPM
entry.content:
Impressive work @Shrenath1903 !

Your hard work, dedication, and passion really stand out in this blog. Integrating Hugging Face AI with SAPUI5 and CAPM shows great intelligence and innovation.

Truly inspiring—keep up the great work!

entry.title: Re: SLED planning PPDS-Order conversion
entry.content:
When you convert a planned order to a production order in RRP3, the order is technically created in ECC/S4, but the batch assignment is not automatically transferred. Standard integration only passes quantities, dates, and pegging relationships,  not the detailed batch proposal. Batch determination in execution is designed to run again in S/4HANA PP at order release, which is why you see the system ignoring the PP/DS batch suggestion.

Activate CIF settings for batch transfer and implement a BAdI during order conversion to ensure pegged batch numbers are copied into the production order. This way, you won’t need to re-run batch determination in S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Employee History Rule – Custom MDF Objec...
entry.content:
Hi @ln000 

Yes, there are known issues about using MDF in employee history.

I would need to test a bit more based on recent enhancements but in a nutshell and as far as I know from my latest testing: 

- there needs to be a record in your mdf for each employee as of the start of your varpay program start date for things to work. 

- If you map a varpayemphist to an MDF field you will have issues in the records retrieved when you run an "import employee history from EC" job. For example if an employee terminates it will not pick up the exact records you want.

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: SAP Joule Copilot responses issue for BP or Sales process in S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hi Experts/Gurus,

I have recently enabled Joule Copilot specifically joule base within S/4HANA Private Cloud edition 2023 version. It works fine for informational & navigational scenarios. However, when i am testing it for transactional capabilities like creating business partner or creating sales order with reference. It doesn't work as expected and it's hard to figure out what's the root cause as all standard roles & backend OData are active.

I am attaching screenshots as of now but i can give more details going forward if someone will engage on this post.
In sales order creation process, it asked for sales order type which i gave but then it stopped, no response at all but then i tried to ask simple information like when this sales order was created but it throws this error that its taking long time to get details from backend.
In BP process, it looks like there is no app for creating BP but i do have access of Manage Business Partner master data. So, hard to understand what went wrong here as well.
Joule SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
@

entry.title: Re: How do l implement a user exit or badi to retrigger r...
entry.content: The challenge is that the standard release strategy logic only retriggers on increases (net price, net value, quantity). If you want it to retrigger on decreases as well, you need to extend the release strategy determination logic with a custom enhancement. Implement logic in BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST or workflow-related BAdIs via the Custom Logic app in Public Cloud. In your code, explicitly check for both increases and decreases in net price, net value, or quantity, and reset the release strategy accordingly.

entry.title: How to delete On-Prem system

How to delete On-Prem system

entry.title: Re: How to delete On-Prem system

This document outlines the necessary steps and considerations to safely proceed with the deletion of On-prem systems while maintaining the integrity of active incidents.

entry.title: Re: SAP Note 3413099 – DDIC activation errors – DRC Franc...
entry.content: First, try manual activation in SE11/SE14 for the missing tables. If blocked by authorization, raise an SAP Incident with reference to Note 3413099. After activation, re-run the note implementation to clear dependent object errors.

entry.title: IAS activation email not triggered after 2nd rehire check
entry.content:
Hello all, 

Has anyone come across IAS issues with welcome email not being triggered after 2nd rehire check and ONB is restarted for the new hire? The SF standard ONB emails get triggered, but there is no new IAS welcome email to activate their account after the rehire match. As per this KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3585126/E, there should be a 2nd activation email or password reset email of some sort triggered from IAS, but this isn't happening for the client.

I have a High priority ticket with SAP and it's been with their engineering team for a week now with no update, hoping someone here has an answer or can at least point me in the right direction.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Query on Multiuser Login in the Custom MDK Client for...
entry.content: Thank you @bill_froelich. It has been two months now. We have already gone live with the custom branded MDK client along with Mirata Digital Forms integration. Multi-user login is working normally, and no major issues have been observed so far. In a few cases, we observed the “.udb - BAD_ENCRYPTION_KEY (-840) error”. I connected with you on a call through an SAP OSS incident regarding this issue.

entry.title: Re: How can Ariba outbound web services directly export d...
entry.content:
We use the Using Web Services to Export Payment Requests function to push data to an iFlow on BTP. The detailed steps are as follows:
 

Step 1: Configure Parameters in Ariba Intelligent Configuration Manager

 
Set up the required parameters in Ariba Intelligent Configuration Manager to enable the payment request web service export.
 

Step 2: Configure the Endpoint and Start the Web Service

 
Define the target endpoint URL pointing to the BTP iFlow, then activate and start the corresponding web service in Ariba.
 

Step 3: Create and Deploy the iFlow on BTP

 
Develop, configure, and deploy the integration flow (iFlow) in SAP BTP Cloud Integration to receive and process the payment request data sent from Ariba.

Step 4: Create actual business data in Spend Management to trigger the WebService

 
Create and submit real payment request–related business transactions in SAP Ariba Spend Management. After approval, the system will automatically trigger the configured WebService and push payment request data to the BTP iFlow endpoint.
 
(After triggering, you can check the integration logs in Intelligent Configuration Manager and view the WebService status in Import/Export monitoring.)

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content: ZAC_2080808 - Week 3 completed

entry.title: Re: Selection production version during the order creatio...
entry.content:
Why Simi-001 picks L002 but Simi-002 defaults to L001 is because, for Simi-001, both validity and lot size conditions allow L002, so the system matches the parent’s version. For Simi-002, either the validity period or lot size of L002 does not match, or the customizing sequence prioritizes L001. That’s why the system consistently falls back to L001.

Check the following:

  • Validity dates of Simi-002’s L002 version — is it active on the order date?
  • Lot size ranges — does L002 cover the order quantity?
  • Selection strategy (OPPQ) — is L002 prioritized for BOM usage 1?
  • Consistency of production version assignment — ensure both phantoms have versions defined correctly in C223/C223N.

entry.title: Re: Adobe LifeCycle designer shows blank page
entry.content: Last update: the blank page appears as I have not admin rights on my computer. As soon as I rund SAP Gui as admin I can see the form even in display mode.

entry.title: Re: How to copy route from sales order into delivery
entry.content:
No solution here without development.

delivery type can be configured to perform route determination during creation. But the function to copy manual route from sales order into delivery is not possible without devleopment. Questions closed.

entry.title: Engineering Agentic AI: A Developer’s Guide to 6 Essential Design Patterns
entry.content:
Treating the LLM as an engine within a broader architectural system, we can design software "agents" that reason, utilize tools, and collaborate. However, moving from a single LLM to a multi-agent system introduces significant software engineering challenges regarding state management, latency, cost, and debugging. There is no "one size fits all" architecture.

Choosing the right design pattern is critical to balancing your system's speed, accuracy, and flexibility. Based on established architectural guidelines, this post provides a comprehensive engineering breakdown of the six core agentic design patterns. We will explore the concepts, technical trade-offs for each, moving from fundamental single-agent setups to advanced orchestration patterns.

Building effective Agentic AI systems is an exercise in managing constraints, here is a detailed look at the 6 patterns you need to know to architect your solution correctly.

1. Single Agent Pattern
The Concept: The Single Agent pattern is the most fundamental architecture. A single Large Language Model (LLM) acts as the sole brain, processing the user prompt, utilizing any provided tools, and generating the final response. It does not hand off tasks to other agents.
When to use it: Simple prototypes, straightforward Q&A, or tasks where you don’t need complex routing or rigorous quality checks.
Pros: Simple and highly flexible.
Cons: Lack of control, it can hallucinate or fail if the task is too complex for one prompt.

single agent pattern

2. Sequential Agent Pattern
The Concept:
In this pattern, multiple specialized agents are chained together in a strict, linear order. The output of the first agent becomes the input for the second agent, and so on.
When to use it: Structured workflows where tasks must happen in a specific order (e.g., Extract Data -> Summarize Data -> Translate Summary).
Pros: Reliable and orderly, easy to track exactly where a failure occurs.
Cons: Inflexible. If step 1 fails, the whole pipeline stalls.

sequential-workflow

3. Parallel Agent Pattern
The Concept:
Instead of waiting for one agent to finish before starting the next, this pattern assigns multiple independent sub-tasks to different specialized agents simultaneously. Once all agents finish, their outputs are aggregated.
When to use it: When tasks are independent and speed is critical (e.g., researching a topic across three different databases at once).
Pros: Fast and highly efficient.
Cons: Higher compute costs since you are running multiple LLM calls concurrently.

parallel agents pattern

4. Loop & Critic Pattern
The Concept:
This is an iterative refinement workflow. A "Generator" agent creates an initial output (like a trip plan). A second "Critique" agent evaluates that output against strict criteria. If the criteria aren't met, the Critique agent sends feedback back to the Generator to try again. This loops until the conditions are met or a max iteration limit is reached.
When to use it: Quality-critical tasks with non-negotiable constraints (e.g., "The hotel must be less than 30 minutes from the event venue").
Pros: Ensures the output meets specific criteria and quality standards.
Cons: Higher latency and cost due to multiple iterations. Risk of infinite loops if exit conditions aren't designed carefully.

Loop critic pattern

5. Coordinator (Router) Pattern
The Concept:
Think of the Coordinator as a smart project manager. When a user submits a complex request, the Coordinator agent analyzes it, breaks it down into sub-tasks (hierarchical task decomposition), and dynamically routes those tasks to a team of specialized sub-agents. The sub-agents take full control of their assigned pieces.
When to use it: Dynamic routing for highly complex problems that require different expertise depending on the user's prompt.
Pros: Extremely flexible and capable of handling wide-ranging user intents.
Cons: Complex to debug, and requires extra model calls (and costs) just for routing the task.

orchestrator pattern

6. Agent as Tool Pattern
The Concept:
On the surface, this looks like the Coordinator pattern, but the core difference lies in control and state. In a Coordinator pattern, the manager hands off the project, and the sub-agent takes over. In the Agent as Tool pattern, the primary agent retains full control. It calls a sub-agent like a stateless tool or function (like a craftsman picking up a hammer, using it, and putting it down), retrieves the specific data it needs, and continues managing the overall state itself.

When to use it: When you need a central agent to maintain context over a long interaction but still need specialized help for stateless sub-tasks.
Pros: Full control and state management remain with the primary agent.
Cons: Requires careful architectural design to ensure the main agent doesn't get overwhelmed with context.

agent as tool pattern

Building effective Agentic AI systems isn't about finding the single "best" pattern, it's about choosing the right pattern for your specific constraint.

  1. Speed? Go Parallel.
  2. Accuracy? Go Loop & Critic.
  3. Flexibility? Use a Coordinator.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere – Replication Flow – Loading delta data from SAP S/4HANA – Q & A Session

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere – Replication Flow – Loading delta data from SAP S/4HANA – Q & A Session
entry.content: Note 2521884 (cds view corresponding BW extractor) commented in the atteched document doesn't exist. Could you tell us the correct note number?

entry.title: Re: How to force exact result recoding within QM Inspecti...
entry.content:
Hi, Solution found.

within certification profile I had to change characteristic details.

Within the characteristic details we needed to change the Fiel text element within Output Control from 0003 - Value and Lower Limit into 0001 - Value and Two Limits.

Quiet tricky.

entry.title: Representation multi-stage processes without POSC in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Warehouse Management)
entry.content:
How to map a classic Goods Receipt process with integrated Quality Inspection and Deconsolidation? 

or

How to configure the Multi Stage process in Warehouse Management?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Representation multi-stage processes without POSC in...
entry.content:
This requirement can be fulfilled by scope items. 
Kindly refer to the SAP For Me scope item link - https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2602/SolP/3M0?region=DE
Here we can find the scope item for "Quality Management in Warehousing (3M0)", which will explain the scenario of multi-stage process in warehouse management. You can refer to the section of "Test script" which will explain the detailed information on how this scope item can be implemented in the system in order to achieve your requirement.

entry.title: Re: SAP Note 3726893 Impact on SAP Commerce Cloud 2211-jd...
entry.content:
Hello,

It use 6.2.10, so should not affected by this bug.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/75d4c3895cb346008545900bffe851ce/25d59f243948472d9d4987a196245755.html 

Let me share some more background for this issue:
1.This issue is caused by a spring bug getPubliclyAccessibleMethodIfPossible() returns hidden static method 
2.Review the source coding for https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.10  which used by 2211-jdk21.5. The bug already exists. (Same as 6.2.11)
3.The bug is be fixed via 6.2.12.
So the version 2211-jdk21.5 (which on  6.2.10) is also be impacted by this issue. Please consider upgrade it to new version.

Best regards,
Kevin

entry.title: Re: How can I query a customers balance with an API? App-ID F0703
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: BKP – Alternative Project Solution for Classical Intercompany Returns (2F4)
entry.content:
Context

You have used intercompany sales solution for your business, corespondingly, returns intercompany solution is needed.

Case 1: In sales, if the classical intercompany sales (scope item 1MX, or 1HO) is used, classical intercompany returns (scope item 2F4) should be the first solution to handle corresponding intercompany returns.

Case 2: In sales, if the advanced intercompany sales (scope item 5D2) is used, classical intercompany returns (scope item 2F4) would be a good short-term solution to handle corresponding intercompany returns, if applicable (5W2 group valuation is not activated). Check more other potentials at BKP - Customer Returns Project Solution for Advanced Intercompany Sales

For classical intercompany returns (2F4), it handles direct shipment of returned product. The solution is compact, integrated, and can fulfill your regular business needs.  

Meanwhile, if you have below special business needs, 2F4 solution can’t handle them well. In short term, there is no enhancement considered.

Case 1: Finance or legal requirement: During goods receipt, returned product should not be valuated (meaning, do not take it back), valuation should take place later such as the logistic follow up decision made during inspection. This is not supported. With solution 2F4, returned product is automatically valuated during post goods receipt in receiving company and the sales cost is reversed. This is by design.

Case 2: Internal credit memo when material inspection split happens. This is not supported. If there is material inspection split for one return item, it is not possible to trigger the internal credit memo creation. System gives an error and blocks the creation. This is the function gap today.

If the above two issues are critical in your business, you can consider an alternative project solution to deal with your intercompany returns business. Customer Returns (BKP) gives your enough process and implementation flexibility.

Standard Solution: 2F4 Classical intercompany returns  

In general, the classical intercompany returns process is a simplified, out of box solution. Step by step guidelines are available in scope item 2F4 .

Classic Intercompany Returns - Single Level / Single System

Below is the classical intercompany returns process flow. This is available in returns overview at item level in manage customer returns version 2 App.

Alternative Solution - Using Logistical Follow-Up Activity (LFU) 0007 – Directly ship to supplier

In general, this is a customized project solution based on customer returns (BKP)

In this solution, basically, we split the returns process for selling company and delivering company. Each company runs BKP (customer returns) separately. It is not one integrated solution.  We utilize the predefined logistical follow-up activity (LFU) 0007 “directly ship to supplier” in the selling company.  This solution option has the following highlights:

  • We establish an internal customer (selling company) and internal supplier (delivering company) relationship - Treating delivering company as the supplier for selling company. 
  • In this return’s scenario, product is returned to delivering plant (delivering company) directly, this is the same as solution in 2F4 classical intercompany returns.
  • Enhanced document flow - returns purchase order and supplier credit memo in selling company, and intercompany returns order in delivering company are created.
  • Inventory, sales cost and revenue can be reversed with regular finance accounting in the two companies.

Selling company handles the returns from the end customer as follows:

  1. Create a returns order with reference as usual or without reference. Use logistical follow-up activity 0007 “Direct ship to supplier”, input the business partner: BP of the delivery company code (not the delivery plant) as supplier, select the right return-to address from the identified supplier. Change the plant to the plant of selling company, instead of the copied intercompany plant.
  2. No returns delivery to the selling company – the customer sends the goods to delivery company directly.
  3. A returns purchase order for the delivering company is created following step 1.
  4. Receive a credit memo from the delivering company and perform supplier credit memo verification accordingly.
  5. Determine refund for the end customer with credit memo request and credit memo.

Delivering company handles (internal) customer returns from selling company as follows:

  1. Create a returns order manually to handle (intercompany) customer returns using the BP of selling company as the sold-to party. Creation with reference is not possible. You should use refund control – decide later if you want to skip the product valuation during goods receipt.
  2. The returns delivery is created, and post goods receipt once the end customer ships back the product. As this is the regular customer returns (BKP) – receive into plant scenario for delivering company, by default, the received product is non-valuated. There is no finance posting during post goods receipt.
  3. Perform product inspection and decide the final logistical follow-up as usual. Material inspection split can be applied here.
  4. Determine the refund for the internal customer – selling company with credit memo request and credit memo.  If there is material inspection split, it is also supported to have split refund in manage customer returns version 2 App.

Master Data Set Up for the Alternative Solution

There are some tips to highlight for the alternative solution. 

  • Use the BP of delivery company code as the supplier. Do not use the BP of the delivering plant.
  • For supplier master data, ensure the returns supplier is selected. With selection, you must assign a customer (same partner with customer role) to deal with the outbound shipping for returns purchase order.

  • You might maintain the right return–to address for the supplier.  Check the help document Return-to Address Determination for Supplier Returns
  •  Ensure you have the right info record maintained for the returns purchase order.

Summary

2F4 classical intercompany returns can be a first consideration as it is compact, integrated, out of box solution, if it fulfills the regular business.

If product valuation should be avoided during goods receipt, or material inspection split happens often, then the alternative project solution with LFU 0007 – Directly ship to supplier would be recommended.

Markus

entry.title: Unable to create Package in very first part of LJ Acquiring Core ABAP Skills and no error message
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to start on the Acquiring Core ABAP Skills Learning Journey and in the very beginning ("Learning Basic ABAP Programming" --> "Understanding Software Structure and Logistics") I am asked to create a new Package in the Superpackage ZSTUDENTS.

However, when I try to do this on the practice system I am enrolled in, I only get as far as the View in the Screenshot; when I click on "Next", nothing happens: no error message, no change in the View. Could you help me understand the problem?

Thanks! Sid

entry.title: What are best guidelines to handle repsonse parameters of ABAP EML for MODIFY and COMMIT
entry.content:
Consider I have the below EML where we can see there is a header and its composition table

modify

entity R_FunctionalLocationTP

create

auto fill cid with lt_floc

create by \_classification

auto fill cid with lt_floc_class

mapped data(ls_mapped)

failed data(ls_failed)

reported data(ls_reported).

 

commit entities

response of R_FunctionalLocationTP

failed final(ls_commit_failed)

reported final(ls_commit_reported).

 

Now imagine the scenario, assuming that we have 10 header records and each having 3 child records

1) out of 10 header records - 2 are in ls_failed but they are not failed ls_commit_failed

2) If an item record is successful and is populated in ls_mapped but failed in commit and is populated in ls_commit_failed.

During this, the relevant header record is in both the mapped structures (modify and commit) .

But since its a composition and because the item record is failed, the header does not get committed to the database (rightly so) but the header doesnt gets populated in any of the failed tables.

So considering multiple scenarios like this, what is the best practice to use these response parameters of both MODIFY and COMMIT ENTITIES, such that I can display the log to the user correctly in detail about each header and item level success or failed messages.

entry.title: TRM: DBT_B008 Planned outflow unexpected amount
entry.content:
Hello, SAP,

 

We have a concern with TRXN: PHONE_NUMBER1 - Cocode: LU19 (Local currency: EUR), related to Investment product type. 

 

Nominal amount: 8M USD 

Start date of the contract was December 30,DIGITS wherein the FX Rate on that day for USD/EUR is 0.ZIPCODE

Supposedly, the contract is until DIGITS. However, an early repayment was processed last June 24, DIGITS with FX rate = 0.ZIPCODE 

However, a derived business transaction DBT_B008 was posted. 

Per checking, DBT_B008 is supposed to output amount with the difference of the Local currency from the initial posting to the local currency of final payment. 

Upon computation, pls refer to attached file, the amount posted in DBT_B008 is not expected.

 

However, that seems not the expected outcome. 

 

Please see attach


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

1 Manage Financial transactions app 

Trxn number: PHONE_NUMBER1 

LU19 

 

2 Check in Treasury posting journal app 



--- Issue Date (UTC) ---
DIGITS-03-08 16:58:42 - DIGITS-03-08 16:58:42

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: TRM: DBT_B008 Planned outflow unexpected amount
entry.content:
We could see the Entries from 2025 and focus on Update Type V202/V203. 

If the G/L Account Long Text mentioned that "Gain........", we could set the amount for Positive.

If the G/L Account Long Text mentioned that "Loss........", we could set the amount for Negative.

At last, add all the positive and negative values together, and then add the DBT_008 Flow. This combined sum represents the expected value for DBT_008, which is the correct system behavior.

entry.title: Re: Parallel line merge to main line not allowed
entry.content:
Hi @SauMaris 

if you have already perform the validations and no open errors remain on your end i would recommend to open a customer case so that the support team checks the system and identifies the reason for the error message.

It is impossible to do this without accessing the system as long as you confirm the rebase was already executed recently without errors. 

Regards,

Lucian

Lucian

entry.title: Re: Direct GR into Special Stock O for Different Vendor
entry.content:
Hello, we have had the same issue. With 2602 it went in. It is possible to place vendor B and tick the special stock indicator in the PO for vendor A. With GR the goods are posted in subcontracting stock of vendor B.

Have a look to the what's new documentation S&P of 2602 release.

regards
Dieter

entry.title: Re: Status of Occupational Health in SAP S/4HANA for EHS
entry.content: Yes as far as my understading is, its back in the portfolio.

entry.title: Object Release State in Cloudification Repository Viewer for Clean Core Extensibility
entry.content:
Introduction

The updated Clean Core Extensibility whitepaper was introduced in August 2025 by SAP as an evolution of the 3-Tier Extensibility concept. This updated the 3-Tier Extensibility to a more detailed assessment of classic ABAP extensions based on the clean core level concept, allowing you as SAP customers and partners stepwise transformation to clean core at your own pace .

While using external APIs in your ABAP custom code, one of the first things to check as per the updated level concept is the release state of that API in the Cloudification Repository Viewer. The release state tells you how stable the object is, what contractual guarantee SAP provides for it, and how your ATC checks will treat any usage of it. Getting this right is important because it directly affects your clean core compliance and your upgrade readiness.

SAP categorizes all objects in the Cloudification Repository Viewer into five distinct states. Each state carries a specific meaning for your custom code, and knowing the difference will help you make better decisions when selecting or replacing APIs.

The Five Release States in the Cloudification Repository Viewer

Here is what each state means in practice and what you should do when you encounter it.

Released

A Released API is one that SAP has formally published with a stability contract. Backward compatibility is guaranteed across upgrades, so you can build on these objects with confidence. Released APIs correspond to clean core Level A.

Level A objects are 100% Cloud compliant, 100% Clean core, can be achieved On-Stack, Side-by-Side or Hybrid.

If you are starting new development, Released APIs are your first choice. They represent the safest and most future-proof option available to you.

Deprecated

A Deprecated API was once Released but SAP is now phasing it out. It still functions today, but it has a planned end of life. SAP recommends that you do not start any new development on it.

If your existing code already uses a Deprecated object, that code is treated as clean core Level B and flagged as Priority 3 in ATC checks. Make sure to identify the successor API in the Cloudification Repository or the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and plan your migration. The sooner you move away from a Deprecated API, the less remediation work you will face when the object reaches its end-of-life date.

Classic API

Classic APIs are well-known, time-tested objects such as BAPIs and RFC-enabled function modules that SAP product experts nominated before a formal release process existed. They do not carry a formal stability contract, but they are upgrade stable and widely used across the SAP ecosystem.

Classic APIs correspond to clean core Level B and are flagged as Priority 3 in ATC checks. Keep in mind that while these objects are stable in practice, they do not carry the same forward-compatibility guarantee as Released APIs. Use them when no Released alternative is available and check the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the availability of the Released APIs as successors to Classic APIs.

Not to be Released

An API marked as Not to be Released is one that SAP has explicitly decided is not intended for external use. Any exemption for using such an object must include a migration plan toward a Released API.

These objects correspond to clean core Level C depending on the context and are flagged as Priority 2 in ATC checks. If you encounter this state, treat it as a signal to find a supported alternative as quickly as possible.

No API

An object classified as No API has no API contract whatsoever. Objects classified as noAPI must not be used, creates maximum technical debt.

This state is flagged as Priority 1 in ATC checks and must be addressed at the highest priority. If you find a noAPI object in your custom code, replacing it with a Released API must be your immediate next step.

Why Some APIs Are Listed as Both Released and Classic or Not to be Released and Classic

You may come across a scenario in the Cloudification Repository Viewer where the same API appears under both the Classic API and Released API categories or Not to be Released and Classic. This is a valid situation and worth understanding, because it can cause some confusion at first glance.

 

Take the CDS view I_EARMARKEDFUNDSDOCUMENTITEM as an example. If you check this object in the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the 2022 SP0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private, it is listed only as a Classic API. When you switch to the 2025 FPS01 release, it appears as both Classic API and Released API.

 

 

What this tells you is that I_EARMARKEDFUNDSDOCUMENTITEM was formally Released in the 2025 FPS0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private.

Both statuses are equally valid for this object. It carries the historical stability of a Classic API and now also has a formal stability contract as a Released API. In this case, the clean core status will be Level A as Released API will take the precedence and ATC check will not give any message.

Similarly, take the Class CL_HTTP_UTILITY as an example. If you check this object in the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the 2022 SP0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private, it is listed only as a Classic API. When you switch to the 2025 FPS0 release, it appears as both Classic API and Not to be Released, with a successor.

Both statuses are equally valid for this object. It carries the historical stability of a Classic API and now also has a formal stability contract as a Not to be Released, with a successor.

In this case, the clean core status will be Level B as Classic API will take the precedence and ATC check will give a Priority 3 message. You are recommended to move to the successor API in this case.

This is why it is recommended to check the Cloudification Repository Viewer against the specific release you are working with.

Conclusion

The Cloudification Repository Viewer continues to grow with each release as SAP adds more objects and updates their states. Use it as your starting point before selecting any API in your custom code. Checking the release state upfront will save you significant remediation work during upgrade cycles and help you stay clean core compliant from day one.

We will continue to share updates on clean core extensibility as the tooling and API landscape evolves. In the meantime, refer to the Cloudification Repository Viewer and the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for the latest state of the objects relevant to your development.

entry.title: Re: Provision required to change the shift factor and var...
entry.content:
Good day @ijlsanthosh 

Based on the little detail you provided in your request, here is response.

Yes, you can change the values for assets before running depreciation, but not as single monthly mass maintenance directly inside the Manage Fixed Assets tile for all assets at once.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/6d3dde531ed3424de10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

For one asset, you can maintain these values in Manage Fixed Assets. For broader recurring changes across many assets, you can use mass processing for asset changes, and you may also consider APIs for mass change in Public Cloud.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/74ffc024fd794eeca49dc8ffe7f9870d/5061f0380ade499f89f26e5cec2e6c36.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/74ffc024fd794eeca49dc8ffe7f9870d/761a00e5c2294f6b87d7392c7d14687a.html?locale=en-US

Another thing you should note is that changing these fields only has an effect if the depreciation key is configured to us multiple shift depreciation logic. Alos, depreciation amount is driven by the depreciation key settings, and the shift factor plus variable portion are part of the multiple shift depreciation logic.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a19cc2531bb9b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/6d3dde531ed3424de10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

So, to answer your request directly:

  1. It is possible for individual assets. In Manage Fixed Assets, you can maintain a new time dependent interval and enter Shift Factor, Portion of Variable Depreciation. This is standard behaviour for depreciation for multiple shift operation.
  2. For the entire plant and machinery population every month, you should handle this through mass processing/upload or API based mass change, not by opening assets one by one in the tile. See KBA 3625447 for more insights.
  3. If the value must apply for the current month, create the new valid from interval before you run the depreciation posting job for that month. If you have already posted, the system requires a new time interval instead of overwriting the historical one. See KBA 2942468 for more insights.

 

My recommendation is:

  • You should use Manage Fixed Assets (New Version) for a few assets.

  • For all plant and machinery assets each month, use Mass Processing for Assets or the Fixed Asset master data API.
  • Ensure the relevant depreciation key is configured for multiple shift depreciation, otherwise the monthly maintenance will not affect the depreciation result as expected.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: How to force measurement point entry in PM order confirmation?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we assigned measuerment points to some of our functional locations to track run time for liquids ran through a pipe for example.

Is there a possibility to force the user to enter actual status of the measurement point (actual counter) during PM order confirmation or at least during completion of the PM order?

We have S4CORE (SAPK-10701INS4CORE).

As of now I did not found anything excepts development what I cannot believe that this is the only way.

thanks in advance.

Best regards

Patrick

entry.title: Submit Royalties

Looks like I have all rights in the portal. Problem is that I am User Administrator, but I may not change my rights. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Submit Royalties
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Kindly know the mentioned Sales & Marketing > Royalties for OEM is a partner related dashboard.

Sorry that we SAP for Me support team cannot assist with any Partner dashboard issue or any partner related topics in SAP for Me.

Could you pls kindly refer to KBA 3165784 and contact the Partner Support team via the "Get Partnership Support" on the partner "Get assistance" dashboard? Sorry for the inconvenience here.

One think can be confirmed from S-user management point of view is that it's correct that you cannot edit your own S-user authorizations, even as user admin. Kindly refer to KBA 2399897.

Hope the above information helps. 

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Can someone explain the difference between segment ve...
entry.content:
Why the times differ is because of the following:

1) Segment scheduling (CA03 / routing simulation)
Uses the routing data directly: setup = 9 min, processing = 2 min → total 11 min. This is a straightforward calculation without considering resource calendars or backward scheduling logic.

2) Latest dates scheduling in PP/DS
PP/DS tries to schedule operations backward from the requirement date using the resource calendar. It applies finite scheduling rules, which can stretch times slightly due to:

  • Bucket alignment (rounding to the resource’s time granularity).
  • Finite capacity consideration (shifting to avoid overlaps).
  • Calendar effects (even if you don’t use explicit breaks, the resource calendar may still impose intervals).
  • Scheduling method differences (latest dates vs. earliest dates).

That is why you see 10:36 min setup and 2:22 min processing — the system is distributing the times differently to fit into the backward scheduling logic.

Perform the below to control it

1) Check resource calendar in PP/DS (transaction /SAPAPO/RES01)
Ensure there are no hidden breaks or intervals. Even if you don’t use “exact breaks,” the calendar definition can still cause shifts.

2) Check scheduling settings in PP/DS
In /SAPAPO/CDPSC2 (PP/DS scheduling parameters), verify whether “latest dates scheduling” is active. You can switch to earliest dates scheduling or finite/infinite scheduling depending on business requirements.

3) Segment scheduling in PP/DS
There is no separate “segment scheduling” switch, but you can simulate routing times in PP/DS using order scheduling with earliest dates to replicate CA03 behavior. Alternatively, use PP/DS detailed scheduling heuristics (e.g., SAP001) to force exact routing times.

4) Utilization rate
You already ruled out the 85% utilization factor, but keep in mind PP/DS can apply efficiency factors if defined in the resource master.

So in nutshel, the summary is the discrepancy comes from PP/DS backward scheduling logic and resource calendar alignment. To control it, check your PP/DS scheduling parameters and resource calendars, and if you want PP/DS to mirror CA03 exactly, use earliest dates scheduling or adjust the resource to infinite capacity without bucket alignment.

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay - Background History
entry.content: @ DavidSomelofske I have try this many times, but not work.

entry.title: Re: SAP Note 3726893 Impact on SAP Commerce Cloud 2211-jd...
entry.content: Checking it internally

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Search Project ... Project manager + WBSs...
entry.content:
Hello @Egbert_de_Jong, 

We have been "experimenting" quite a bit with defining the enterprise project manager role ourselves 😄 So here some ideas that come to my mind.

 

One reason why project managers can not find the projects while finance can could be the assignment of different catalogues. I will demonstrate this on two BR standard roles:

vs.

Catalogue SAP_PPM_BC_PROJ_MGMT_PC in the Project Manager role contains a filter “Based on Project Responsibility” while catalogue SAP_PS_BC_PROJ_CONTRL_MC in the Project Financial Controller role doesn’t contain such a filter. Therefore, a Project Manager will have a limited view. If the Project Manager is not properly set up as a Project Manager for a specific project, the PM will not be able to see it. See Access Control for Enterprise Projects | SAP Help Portal for more info.

 

Another idea is that the Project Manager role might have the non-limited catalogue SAP_PS_BC_PROJ_CONTRL_MC assigned but the relevant apps from this catalogue are de-activated. This is for example the case in our custom role.

 

To find out, whether the Project Manager role is lacking some catalogues or apps I would suggest the following two options:

 

1. Compare the two roles in the App Maintain Business Roles and analyse the differences

 

2. Use the App Display Authorization Trace to trace a user with the Project Manager role and then analyse which Authorization Checks failed. These usually contain an info what is missing in the users’ roles. See Display Authorization Trace | SAP Help Portal for more info.

 

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: Selection production version during the order creatio...
entry.content: Hi，Thanks for your answer, but for the L002, and L001, we have the same valid date, I tried to change the Order component : Simi-001 1 ea, Simi-002 -1 Ea, Simi-002 1 ea... simi-002 with 1 ea will have the L002 version. while the negative one still keep the L001 Version.

Simi-002 (L001/L002)  -1 EA

Simi-002 (L001/L002) 1 EA

entry.title: Re: Automatic creation of reports in S4Hana Public Cloud
entry.content: As of 2602, there is no way to do so. However, an improvement request has been raised: Improvement Request 361434 - Automatic creation of reports in S4Hana Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: Status of Occupational Health in SAP S/4HANA for EHS
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, as of S/4HANA release 2025 for private cloud and on-premise, Occupational Health has been fully merged into the S/4 EHS workplace safety solution as standard capability. No additional RCS solution is needed.

Best regards,

Leyi
SAP EHS Product Marketing Manager

entry.title: Re: [SF] (Single Sign-On) SSO for SAP SuccessFactors

entry.title: Re: Selection production version during the order creatio...
entry.content: they have the same date and all valid. same material with positive will get the right version. but negative will get wrong one

entry.title: MRP Live taking more time for execution with single material as well , need to understand reasons ?
entry.content:
Hello All ,

This question is more specific to MRP Live performance , I need to understand when I am running single material (header ) which having mostly 10 BOM components taking  more than 5 mins for displaying MRP run results .

What are the factors responsible for MRP live execution delays , how I can improve this situation ?

For example , if I use 100 materials in the same process , MRP live should take same time .

Question is more technical than functional requirement hence I expect someone from basis should answer it.

entry.title: Re: MultiLevel BOM Report for all Materials
entry.content: Yes, it is feasible in S/4HANA. You can either build a CDS view for real-time BOM hierarchy reporting or create a custom table refreshed by ABAP for stable snapshots. The choice depends on whether you need real-time analytics or periodic frozen data for budgeting.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (Bangalore, India)
entry.content: Looking forward to attending the event.

entry.title: Re: Status of Occupational Health in SAP S/4HANA for EHS
entry.content:
Yes,
 
Occupational Health has been merged into S/4HANA EHS Workplace Safety as standard capability.
 
Therefor a customer does not need to purchase additional licensing of the previous RCS solution in S/4HANA 2025.
 
Also, as part of the roadmap for Environment Health and Safety for the S/HANA Public Cloud, a 'revamped' OH solution is planned for Q3 2026, which normally means that it should be available in two years' time for private and on premise.  OH is currently not available in Public Cloud.
 

 

Thanks!

Could you please guide how to fix this issue or provide an solution.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Re: LGM Knowledge Snippet: What happens when I press the "Print Label" button in the parce
entry.content:
As a short addition: 
Step 5 (Closing the HUs) also updates the packed quantity and status in the Outbound Warehouse Request Item. Once the whole quantity is contained in closed HUs, the packing milestone is shown as completed in the Manage Outbound Warehouse Activities app.

entry.title: No possibility to register FileAdapter in DPAgent 2.8.0 with Datasphere
entry.content:
I have installed and connected DPAgent 2.8.0 with Datasphere. The status is on green in Datasphere and I can register the available Adapters (for instance, ABAP). As it is indicated in note 3487646, it is not indicating the connection to HANA in the Agent with versions over 2.7.4. 

In the list of available Adapters in Datasphere, it is not shown FileAdapter as an option. Then, I try to register it with CLI configuration tool agentcli.bat --configAgent, but the next message appears:

Can anyone help me registering FileAdapter, please?

entry.title: Re: Revaluation of Fixed Asset – Posting in a Separate Do...
entry.content:
Hi,

You are asking for a feature that has been realized in UPA (Universal Parallel Accounting).  In UPA, we have a Fiori app F7775 in addition to the existing transaction code ABAWL. In both applications, you can get your revaluation posted directly, without having to run AFAB, which generates a huge document.

Cheers

María

==========================

entry.title: Customer Partner Function - CDS View
entry.content:
Which CDS view can be used to retrieve the value of the Partner Function Type ? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Customer Partner Function - CDS View
entry.content:
To retrieve the Partner Function Type field, use the following CDS view : Customer Sales Area Partner Function.
Refer this help Documentation for more details : Customer Sales Area Partner Function.

entry.title: Cannot create Vendor with grouping B1C for existing Business Partner
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I am working on a demo version of SAP FICO and need help with a Business Partner issue. A Customer Master Record was successfully created under BP 9921 using grouping B1C, but when attempting to extend the same BP to also function as a Vendor, the following error appears: "You cannot create a vendor with grouping B1C."

Since the BP number is already linked to the Customer Master Record, the grouping cannot be changed.

  1. Is it possible to extend a BP with a customer grouping to also hold a Vendor role?
  2. Should a neutral/combined grouping have been used from the start, and if so which one supports both roles?
  3. Is there any way to reassign the grouping without losing the existing Customer Master Record data?
  4. An Accounts Receivable account was created as a workaround — was this correct or does it need to be reversed?
  5. What is the recommended process for linking both a Customer and Vendor role to the same BP number?

Any help is appreciated, thank you!

entry.title: ABAP Performance Tuning in S/4HANA Series : Part 3 : SAT + ST12
entry.content:
SAT + ST12: Profiling ABAP CPU and Memory the Right Way

Series: ABAP Performance Tuning in S/4HANA | Part 3 of 7 | Author: Manish Khanna

Your ST05 trace is clean. No expensive SQL, no N+1 patterns, indexes in place. But the program still takes 45 seconds. The problem is inside ABAP itself — a loop doing MOVE-CORRESPONDING on a 200-field structure 80,000 times, or a factory method creating a new object instance on every iteration. SAT finds the first problem in 10 minutes. ST12 finds the second.

This post covers both tools and when to use which.

SAT — Finding CPU Hotspots

Transaction: SAT (replaces legacy SE30)

SAT measures CPU time, call counts, and execution time per ABAP statement, function module, method, and form routine. Use it when the database is not the bottleneck.

Running an SAT Trace

Step 1 — Configure

Enter SAT. Click Change Measurement Settings (F6)

 

Setting Recommendation
VariantAll Statements on the first run
Aggregate records✅Enable — raw traces can be millions of rows
Maximum trace records500,000 to start; increase if trace cuts off
Restrict to userYour user ID

💡Tip: For batch jobs, use the Background measurement option. For dialog programs, use Local — traces only your session.

Step 2 — Start, Execute, Stop

Click Start Measurement (F8). Status bar shows "Measurement active". Execute your program or transaction. Return to SAT and click Stop Measurement.

Step 3 — Evaluate

Click Evaluate Last Measurement. The hit list shows rows sorted by gross time descending.

Reading SAT Results

The metric that matters: Net time percentage

Gross time includes all sub-calls. Net time is exclusive to that statement. A method with 80% gross time but 2% net time is innocent — the cost is inside a child call. Sort by net time to find the real bottleneck.

💡Tip: If a single statement consumes more than 20% of net time, investigate it first. Everything else is secondary.

Call count anomalies are often more important than per-call time:

A method at 0.01ms per call sounds harmless. At 80,000 calls, that's 800ms — significant. Look for methods with call counts in the tens of thousands, especially inside loops.

What Each Statement Type Tells You

 

Statement Type High Value Means
SELECTMove to ST05 — the DB layer is the real problem
LOOP ATCheck table type, key access, WHERE condition
CALL METHODObject instantiation inside loops, excessive polymorphism
MOVE-CORRESPONDINGLarge structures with field catalog mismatch
CONCATENATE / string opsString building in a loop — use string tables

Common SAT Findings and Fixes

MOVE-CORRESPONDING in a loop on large structures:

" ❌ Bad: MOVE-CORRESPONDING on 80-field structure, 50,000 times
LOOP AT lt_source INTO ls_source.
  MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_source TO ls_target.
  APPEND ls_target TO lt_target.
ENDLOOP.

" ✅ Good: map only the fields you actually need
LOOP AT lt_source ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<src>).
  DATA(ls_t) = VALUE ty_target(
    vbeln = <src>-vbeln
    posnr = <src>-posnr
    matnr = <src>-matnr ).
  APPEND ls_t TO lt_target.
ENDLOOP.

String concatenation in a loop:

" ❌ Bad: CONCATENATE creates a new string object each iteration
DATA lv_result TYPE string.
LOOP AT lt_lines INTO lv_line.
  CONCATENATE lv_result lv_line INTO lv_result SEPARATED BY ','.
ENDLOOP.

" ✅ Good: build the table first, concatenate once
LOOP AT lt_lines INTO lv_line.
  APPEND lv_line TO lt_parts.
ENDLOOP.
CONCATENATE LINES OF lt_parts INTO lv_result SEPARATED BY ','.

Always use ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL in performance-critical loops:

" ❌ Bad: copies the entire structure per iteration
LOOP AT lt_data INTO ls_data.
  ls_data-status = 'X'.
  MODIFY lt_data FROM ls_data.
ENDLOOP.

" ✅ Good: direct reference, no copy
LOOP AT lt_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs>).
  <fs>-status = 'X'.
ENDLOOP.

ST12 — Adding Memory Analysis

Transaction: ST12

ST12 gives you everything SAT gives you, plus memory consumption tracking per object and class. Use ST12 instead of SAT whenever:

  • The program uses ABAP Objects (classes) extensively
  • You're seeing STORAGE_PARAMETERS_WRONG_SET dumps
  • Memory usage grows over time in long-running sessions

 

 

SAT vs. ST12 — When to Use Which

 

 SAT ✅ST12✅
Runtime analysis✅✅
Memory allocation tracking❌✅
Object instance counting❌✅
Trace file sizeSmallerLarger
Best forPure CPU profilingOO programs, memory problems

Running an ST12 Trace

Enter ST12. Click New Measurement (F6)

 

Option Setting✅
ABAP trace✅Always enable
Memory trace✅Enable
Aggregate identical positions✅Enable
Maximum size (KB)50,000 for most programs

Start measurement, run your program, stop. Double-click the saved measurement.

The Memory Consumption View

Click Memory Analysis. The columns to watch:

 

Column What It Tells You
Allocated (KB)Heap memory allocated by this object
Released (KB)Memory freed (GC, FREE, CLEAR)
Net allocationAllocated minus Released — net growth
InstancesNumber of object instances created

Red flags:

  • Net allocation > 10 MB for a single transaction
  • Instances count in the hundreds of thousands for a class that should have 1–2 instances
  • Net allocation grows each time you call the same transaction — accumulating without release

The Most Common Memory Anti-Patterns

Object instantiation inside a loop — the silent memory killer:

" ❌ Bad: 50,000 instances created and never GC'd efficiently
LOOP AT lt_data INTO ls_data.
  lo_processor = NEW zcl_data_processor( ).
  lo_processor->process( ls_data ).
ENDLOOP.

" ✅ Good: one instance, reused
lo_processor = NEW zcl_data_processor( ).
LOOP AT lt_data INTO ls_data.
  lo_processor->process( ls_data ).
ENDLOOP.

Static class attributes that accumulate across calls:

Static attributes persist for the entire ABAP session lifetime. If a method is called in a loop and pushes to a static cache without eviction, memory grows without bound:

" ❌ Bad: static cache grows unbounded
CLASS zcl_cache DEFINITION.
  CLASS-DATA: mt_cache TYPE HASHED TABLE OF ty_entry
                        WITH UNIQUE KEY key.
  CLASS-METHODS: add_entry IMPORTING is_entry TYPE ty_entry.
ENDCLASS.

" ✅ Good: add a maximum size + eviction policy
CLASS-METHODS: add_entry IMPORTING is_entry TYPE ty_entry.
" In implementation:
" IF lines( mt_cache ) > 10000. DELETE mt_cache ... ENDIF.

Using CL_ABAP_MEMORY_UTILITIES for programmatic monitoring:

DATA(lv_memory_kb) = cl_abap_memory_utilities=>get_current_memory_use( ) / 1024.
WRITE: / 'Current memory (KB):', lv_memory_kb.

Add this before and after the code block you're profiling to measure net growth.

The Combined Workflow

  1. Run ST05 — clean? Good, move on.
  2. Run SAT — find the high-net-time statements.
  3. If you see CALL METHOD with very high call counts, or any OO code: switch to ST12.
  4. In ST12, check both the runtime view (same as SAT) AND the memory view.
  5. Fix one thing. Re-measure. Confirm improvement before the next fix.

Never skip step 5. A change that looks like it should help sometimes makes things worse. The measurement is the proof.

Key Takeaways

  • SAT is the right tool when the database is not the bottleneck
  • Sort by net time, not gross time — gross time includes child calls
  • High call counts in loops are often more impactful than high per-call time
  • Use ST12 instead of SAT for any OO-heavy or memory-intensive program
  • Always use ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL in performance-critical LOOP AT
  • Fix one thing at a time, re-measure after each change

Next in this series: ATC: Make the ABAP Test Cockpit Your Pre-Transport Quality Gate — catch anti-patterns statically before any trace, and integrate ATC into your CI/CD pipeline.

Series Home Page

entry.title: Re: How to find a CDS view for a classic Table in ABAP? - Cheat Sheet
entry.content:
Hello, 

Can you share it again please, I can't access to the link...

entry.title: Re: SAP Public cloud - how to activate cost center in sal...
entry.content:
Good day @nauttawanit 

Thank you for your question.

If you are referring to the standard cost center field at sales order item level,  then you cannot activate this field as a standard item field in Public Cloud in the way you would typically expect from configuration or UI activation. See KBA 3475182 for more insights.

If your goal is to capture or display cost center related information on the sales order item for business purposes, then the Public Cloud supports by allowing you to create a custom field in business sales context: Sales Document Item. Enable it for the sales order UI, and expose it through Adapt UI in Manage Sales Order -Version 2. See KBA 3619567 for more insights.

So, i recommend:

  1. You confirm whether your need is accounting driven or informational.
  2. If it is informational, then create a custom field in Sales: Sales Document Item.
  3. Enable and publish the field.
  4. Add it to the item UI in Manage Sales Orders-Version 2 and test the behaviour in a test sales order.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Datasphere AWS S3 Connection to a DTP Object Store Service not possible
entry.content:
I have an AWS S3 Object Store created on our BTP Account. A Service Key has been created. When I try to validate the connection in Datasphere the next message appears:

Cause: Possible causes of error: Detailed error message (user: arn:aws:iam::'user'  is not authorized to perform: s3:listallmybuckets because no identity\-based policy allows the s3:listallmybuckets action (service: amazon s3; status code: 403; error code: accessdenied; request id: 8bg3wzykpdqw7p1m; s3 extended request id: 'id'.

Seeing this message, I want to try to afford the corresponding policies to the user of the Object Store (as indicated in SAP Datasphere - How to configure the AWS Assume Role option for an AWS S3 connection).

Anyone knows how to do it for a BTP Object Store or is there a limitation?

entry.title: Re: How to use BPA API Trigger using Fiori
entry.content:
Hello @Vennilla00 ,

Could you please share the properties of your sbpa destination which you are using for triggering process from fiori app ?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Q&A section to have Public Cloud or Private Cloud option to choose
entry.content:
Hello @Lakshmipathi you are right, it's often hard to tell which SAP product is being discussed.

And as a regular poster myself, I realize that I often forget to mention in my posts that I'm referring to Public Cloud. A compulsory selection (in addition to the available managed tags) would really be helpful here 🙂

entry.title: PLD Layout Issue – Page Footer Not Visible in Tax Invoice Preview.
entry.content:
In the PLD layout of the Tax Invoice, the page footer is not visible in the layout preview, even after adding it to the Page Footer section.

I have already tried removing and re-adding the footer, but it is still not appearing in the preview.

Could you please advise what settings or configurations need to be checked to make the page footer visible in the layout?

entry.title: Renewing SAP Learning Hub Student Edition Subscription
entry.content:
Renewing your SAP Learning Hub Student Edition subscription? 

You can renew up to 30 days before your expiry date.

Step 1: Log in to your SAP Student Zone using your student email as usual.

Step 2: Visit this link to reverify your institution through SheerID: 👉 Click here to reverify

Your subscription comes with two certification exam attempts. Good luck everyone! 🎓

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere – Replication Flow – Loading delta data from SAP S/4HANA – Q & A Session
entry.content: Hi Norman, Through Analytical Model it is possible but not through a view or table. which is very important for any org.

entry.title: Re: Marketing Cloud - Maintain Employees - missing new bu...

Hi Markus,

thanks for confirming. I added the Create Button but when I click it, the side panel opens but I cannot enter any data...

entry.title: Re: Taking Data Federation to the Next Level: Accessing Remote ABAP CDS View Entities in SAP HANA Cl
entry.content:
Hello @akuller,

As explained in this blog, this feature only supports an ABAP CDS view entity as a remote object, and there are no plans to support other objects directly. Since you can pass parameters to an ABAP CDS view entity through a virtual table, if an AMDP table function is defined as a building block of the ABAP CDS view entity, it can be leveraged indirectly.

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning HUB
entry.content: I purchased a subscription to the SAP Learning Hub in February to gain access to the training systems. However, I did not receive an activation code to activate the license. Neither the Learning Hub team nor the SAP Store team has been able to help me. Any solution for that problem?

entry.title: Re: ST05: Activate Trace Recording with Filter
entry.content: Thank you for this awesome blog series!

entry.title: Re: Switching from D365 FO Consultant to SAP S/4 Hana Fun...
entry.content:
I’ve actually worked on both sides. Earlier with SAP, and now I’m working with a Dynamics 365 partner, so I can relate to your situation.

Your SCM knowledge is definitely transferable. Core processes like procurement, inventory, and planning are very similar across both systems. The challenge is not capability, it’s the lack of SAP experience tag.

From what I’ve seen:
SAP is more structured and deep, but harder to break into. D365 is more flexible and easier to adapt, but SAP roles strongly prioritize prior product experience.

That’s why you’re not getting shortlisted.

If you really want to switch, focus on getting hands-on SAP exposure. Even small project experience matters more than just certifications. Also be prepared for a possible step back initially to enter the SAP ecosystem.

Once you get in, your combined D365 + SAP experience will actually become a strong advantage.

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content: Week 2 challenge completed. Package: ZAC_343570

entry.title: Re: Valuation Category Enable Issue
entry.content:
Dear @venkatachalam47,

The system does not allow changing / activating split valuation (single-batch valuation) once the material has already been used in the plant if valuation-related data still exists. Even if current stock has been issued out and open documents have been closed, SAP may still block the change because valuation-related dependencies may still remain in the system, such as existing valuation types. In related Public Cloud material master checks, SAP also documents that valuated stock in the current or previous period, as well as open purchasing or scheduling dependencies can block such changes.

SAP Public Cloud documentation states that the valuation category can only be changed when the valuation type no longer exists for the material

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3682327

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3558716

Recommended solution options:

  1. Check whether any valuation type still exists for the material and, if yes, set it for deletion / archiving according to SAP guidance
  2. Verify that there is no valuated stock in either the current or the previous period and that no open purchase orders or delivery schedule lines remain.
  3. If historical dependencies cannot be fully removed the safest practical approach is to create a new material or a new plant extension and maintain the valuation setup correctly before any postings. SAP Help indicates that single-batch valuation must be prepared upfront using valuation category X Automatic Batch

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/289644d401a844878ce84670517dfa98.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/9c62e3f7092744d49021051301263e85.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/476cf31fd1e046a0889bc3ef8da325c3.html

Summary:

If the historical valuation dependencies cannot be fully removed the practical workaround is to create a new material or a new plant extension and maintain the valuation setup correctly before any postings. This is an implementation recommendation based on SAP documented restrictions, not a direct SAP sentence. SAP documents that the valuation category can only be changed if the valuation type no longer exists and SAP Help shows that single batch valuation must be prepared with valuation category X an initial valuation type and dependent valuation records before inventory posting

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Marketing Cloud - Maintain Employees - missing new bu...
entry.content: Ah yes, I actually can't enter any data either. Also the Create button keeps disappearing. Probably best to raise an incident then.

entry.title: Re: Supply Protection on the Level of the Storage Location
entry.content:
Hello @Pettmark36,
yes, you are right. As supply protection is integrated in the Product Availability Check the batch must be know there. Only defined batches can be selected in the definition of the planning level.

entry.title: How to show custom field from app: supplier invoice to app: manage supplier line item
entry.content:
I would like to create a custom field in the header section of the Create Supplier Invoice app.

After posting the supplier invoice, I would like this field to be visible in the Manage Supplier Line Items app.

Could you please confirm whether this is possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition)?

entry.title: Re: Can SAP DOCUMENT AI make automatic template selection?
entry.content:
Hi @TMNielsen 
Yes, we do support a "template auto-detect feature" both in UI and API. See e.g. here https://help.sap.com/docs/document-ai/sap-document-ai/add-document
When considering templates, I recommend to also have a look at the best practices and limitations: https://help.sap.com/docs/document-ai/sap-document-ai/template-best-practices

Besides this, our recommendation for such scenarios is to consider the premium edition with instant learning to avoid the overhead of creating and maintaining templates.

Best regards, Tobias

entry.title: Re: ATC Check Error: "Program does not exist"
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check SAP note 3625427 - Addon build pipeline fails - SAP for Me and implement it (or SAP_BASIS SP01). This should help to get rid of these ATC errors.

Best regards,
  Dirk

SAP S/4HANA 2023 (On-Premise)

 

 

Thanks in advance 🙂 - Markus

entry.title: Re: Shipping Invoices, Special Offers and Other Inserts
entry.content:
In most European EPRs printed paper is excluded from EPR reporting.

Items, such as invoices, leaflets, discount coupons, user manuals, magazines, etc. are not considered packaging.

However, the USA Oregon EPR includes printed paper in the scope of what needs to be reported.

The obligations of who needs to report the printed paper are described in the reporting guidance.

RDP has one packaging type, PRP, which is meant to be used for packaging elements made of printed paper.

All SAP-provided reports for Europe have a rule to exclude this packaging type.

If the company will be reporting only in Europe then it doesn't matter if they maintain the coupons and invoices as packaging elements or not.

If the company will report in USA Oregon, then they have to create the printed paper as packaging element.

They may need to adjust the rules from the delivered Oregon template, as the printed paper must be split into several categories.

Even if the company operates only in Europe, it may still decide to create the packaging elements PRP, in case future regulation changes the reporting or if they want to do internal reporting (e.g how much paper do we put on the market for our leaflets, user manuals and discount coupons).

A sustainability-engaged company may want to track these weights and make a case for digitizing the user manuals, measure the positive environmental impact of electronic invoices, etc.

Bottom lines:

  • It's hard to give a general answer - it's best for the company to check the guidance of the respective PRO
  • SAP-provided reports make sure to exclude the printed paper, wherever it is required to be excluded. This is documented in the reporting documentation.
